title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Relational Recall
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.
Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Acid Transactions
steps.instruction:
Order the Acid Transactions evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Acid Transactions.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Acid Transactions assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Acid Transactions, keep Acid Transactions connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Acid Transactions has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
